content.fact_text: Implementation of .env.example configuration template for production deployment.

provenance_data.behaviour: Creates a production-ready environment variable template (.env.example) that serves as the blueprint for required infrastructure configurations, ensuring consistency across distributed instances.
provenance_data.utility: Provides a documentation template for production environment variables, preventing misconfiguration during node deployment.

content.source_code:
# ==============================================================================
# E-COMMERCE BACKEND CONFIGURATION TEMPLATE
# ==============================================================================
# This file serves as the singular source of truth for runtime environment
# configuration. Copy this file to '.env' and populate with production values.
# All fields are mandatory for system initialization.
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# APPLICATION METADATA
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# APP_ENV: The runtime environment (e.g., development, staging, production)
APP_ENV=production
# LOG_LEVEL: The severity threshold for system logging (e.g., debug, info, warn, error)
LOG_LEVEL=info
# PORT: The TCP port the application service will bind to
PORT=3000

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POSTGRESQL DATABASE CONFIGURATION
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DB_HOST: The hostname or IP address of the PostgreSQL primary instance
DB_HOST=REQUIRED_DB_HOST
# DB_PORT: The port number of the PostgreSQL instance
DB_PORT=5432
# DB_USER: The authenticated database user
DB_USER=REQUIRED_DB_USER
# DB_PASSWORD: The secure password for the database user
DB_PASSWORD=REQUIRED_DB_PASSWORD
# DB_NAME: The database instance name
DB_NAME=REQUIRED_DB_NAME
# DB_POOL_MIN: Minimum number of connections in the connection pool (keep low to save resources)
DB_POOL_MIN=2
# DB_POOL_MAX: Maximum number of connections to handle peak concurrency
DB_POOL_MAX=20

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CACHING AND SESSION LAYER (REDIS)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# REDIS_URL: Connection URI for the Redis instance (e.g., redis://user:password@host:port/db)
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0
# REDIS_TTL_DEFAULT: Default Time-To-Live for cached items in seconds
REDIS_TTL_DEFAULT=3600
# REDIS_TIMEOUT: Connection timeout in milliseconds for operations
REDIS_TIMEOUT=5000

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MESSAGE BROKER (KAFKA)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# KAFKA_BROKER_URL: Comma-separated list of bootstrap brokers (e.g., host1:9092,host2:9092)
KAFKA_BROKER_URL=REQUIRED_KAFKA_BROKERS
# KAFKA_CLIENT_ID: Unique identifier for this application client
KAFKA_CLIENT_ID=ecommerce-backend-service
# KAFKA_GROUP_ID: Consumer group identifier for load balancing events
KAFKA_GROUP_ID=ecommerce-consumer-group
# KAFKA_PRODUCER_TIMEOUT: Timeout in milliseconds for message acknowledgment
KAFKA_PRODUCER_TIMEOUT=3000

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECURITY AND IDENTITY MANAGEMENT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# JWT_SECRET: Strong, random secret for signing authentication tokens (min 64 chars)
JWT_SECRET=REQUIRED_SECURE_JWT_SECRET_STRING
# JWT_EXPIRATION: Token expiration time in seconds (e.g., 3600 for 1 hour)
JWT_EXPIRATION=3600
# ARGON2_SALT_ROUNDS: Number of iterations for password hashing (security vs performance)
ARGON2_SALT_ROUNDS=12
